data.content:
### Settings UI Views

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Pareto-Mac/CLAUDE.md

Various views for the application's settings, including an About view displaying app and helper versions, and a Permissions settings view for continuous monitoring.

```swift
// /Pareto/Views/Settings/AboutSettingsView.swift
import SwiftUI

struct AboutSettingsView: View {
    // State variables to hold app and helper versions
    @State private var appVersion: String = "Unknown"
    @State private var helperVersion: String = "Unknown"

    var body: some View {
        VStack {
            Text("App Version: \(appVersion)")
            Text("Helper Version: \(helperVersion)")
        }
    }
}

// /Pareto/Views/Settings/PermissionsSettingsView.swift
struct PermissionsSettingsView: View {
    // Continuous permission monitoring UI elements
    var body: some View {
        Text("Continuous Permission Monitoring")
    }
}
```

--------------------------------

### vibe-tools Configuration Files

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Cursor-Tools/CLAUDE.md

Configuration for vibe-tools can be managed through specific JSON and environment files. These files dictate settings, API keys, and model providers.

```json
vibe-tools.config.json
~/.vibe-tools/config.json
```

```env
.vibe-tools.env
~/.vibe-tools/.env
```

--------------------------------

### Set Perplexity API Key

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Configures the Perplexity API key, either in a .env file or as an environment variable.

```bash
PERPLEXITY_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
```

--------------------------------

### Configure GitHub Token (Environment Variable)

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/scripts/BADGE_AUTOMATION_SETUP.md

Sets the GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) as an environment variable for local testing. This token is required for creating issues in external repositories.

```bash
export AWESOME_CC_PAT_PUBLIC_REPO=your_github_personal_access_token
```

--------------------------------

### Global Rule Configuration

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Cursor-Tools/CLAUDE.md

A global rule configuration that is always applied. It specifies globs for matching files and a flag to ensure it's always active.

```APIDOC
description: Global Rule. This rule should ALWAYS be loaded
globs: _,**/_
alwaysApply: true
```

--------------------------------

### Claude Desktop Configuration (NVM Users)

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Configuration for NVM users, requiring absolute paths for both the Node.js executable and the server script.

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "perplexity": {
      "command": "/Users/username/.nvm/versions/node/v16.x.x/bin/node",
      "args": [
        "/Users/username/path/to/perplexity-mcp/server.js"
      ],
      "env": {
        "PERPLEXITY_API_KEY": "your_perplexity_api_key"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Repository Analysis Configuration

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Cursor-Tools/CLAUDE.md

Customize file inclusion/exclusion for repository analysis using a `repomix.config.json` file. This impacts commands like `repo`, `plan`, and `doc`.

```json
repomix.config.json
```

--------------------------------

### Welcome Screen UI

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Pareto-Mac/CLAUDE.md

The welcome screen view, responsible for checking and displaying permissions, including a specific section for macOS 15+ firewall permissions. Defines window size.

```swift
// /Pareto/Views/Welcome/PermissionsView.swift
import SwiftUI

struct PermissionsView: View {
    // Continuous monitoring logic for permissions
    @StateObject private var permissionsChecker = PermissionsChecker()

    var body: some View {
        VStack {
            // Firewall permission section (macOS 15+ only)
            if #available(macOS 15.0, *) {
                FirewallPermissionView()
            }
            // Other permission checks...
        }
        .frame(minWidth: 450, minHeight: 500)
    }
}

// Placeholder for FirewallPermissionView and PermissionsChecker
struct FirewallPermissionView: View { var body: some View { Text("Firewall Status") } }
class PermissionsChecker: ObservableObject { /* ... */ }
```

--------------------------------

### Claude Desktop Configuration (Local Install)

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Configures the Perplexity MCP server in Claude Desktop when installed locally. Specifies the command to run the server script and its environment variables.

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "perplexity": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": [
        "/absolute/path/to/perplexity-mcp/server.js"
      ],
      "env": {
        "PERPLEXITY_API_KEY": "your_perplexity_api_key"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### XPC Service Configuration

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Pareto-Mac/CLAUDE.md

Configuration plist for the system daemon, specifying the program to execute and its bundle structure. Crucial for system integration.

```plist
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
    <key>Label</key>
    <string>co.niteo.ParetoSecurityHelper</string>
    <key>ProgramArguments</key>
    <array>
        <string>/Library/Application Support/Pareto/ParetoSecurityHelper.app/Contents/MacOS/ParetoSecurityHelper</string>
    </array>
    <key>RunAtLoad</key>
    <true/>
    <key>KeepAlive</key>
    <true/>
    <key>ProcessType</key>
    <string>Background</string>
    <key>StandardOutPath</key>
    <string>/tmp/ParetoSecurityHelper.stdout.log</string>
    <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
    <string>/tmp/ParetoSecurityHelper.stderr.log</string>
    <key>BundleProgram</key>
    <string>Contents/MacOS/ParetoSecurityHelper</string>
</dict>
</plist>
```

--------------------------------

### Claude Desktop Configuration (Global Install)

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Configures the Perplexity MCP server in Claude Desktop after a global npm installation. Uses 'npx' to execute the installed package.

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "perplexity": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "perplexity-mcp"
      ],
      "env": {
        "PERPLEXITY_API_KEY": "your_perplexity_api_key"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshooting: Enable Verbose Logging

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Perplexity-MCP/CLAUDE.md

Enables verbose logging for the Perplexity MCP server by setting the DEBUG environment variable.

```bash
DEBUG=1
```

--------------------------------

### Configure GitHub Token (.env File)

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/scripts/BADGE_AUTOMATION_SETUP.md

Creates a .env file to store the GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT). This is the recommended method for local testing and is gitignored for security.

```bash
echo "AWESOME_CC_PAT_PUBLIC_REPO=your_github_personal_access_token" > .env
```

--------------------------------

### Computer-Use Demo Setup and Development

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/official-documentation/Anthropic-Quickstarts/CLAUDE.md

Commands for setting up the environment, building a Docker image, and running the Computer-Use Demo container. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable.

```shell
./setup.sh
```

```shell
docker build . -t computer-use-demo:local
```

```shell
docker run -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -v $(pwd)/computer_use_demo:/home/computeruse/computer_use_demo/ -v $HOME/.anthropic:/home/computeruse/.anthropic -p 5900:5900 -p 8501:8501 -p 6080:6080 -p 8080:8080 -it computer-use-demo:local
```

--------------------------------

### LLM Configuration Loading

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/README.md

Loads LLM configuration files to context, importing specific terminology, model configurations, and establishing baseline terminology for AI discussions.

```markdown
`/load-llms-txt` by `ethpandaops` (MIT)
Loads LLM configuration files to context, importing specific terminology, model configurations, and establishing baseline terminology for AI discussions.
```

--------------------------------

### API Key Requirements for Commands

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Cursor-Tools/CLAUDE.md

Certain commands within vibe-tools require specific API keys to be set in environment files. This ensures proper authentication with external services.

```env
CLICKUP_API_TOKEN
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
OPENROUTER_API_KEY
```

--------------------------------

### URL Scheme Support

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Pareto-Mac/CLAUDE.md

Custom URL schemes supported by the Pareto Security application for triggering specific actions like resetting settings, showing the menu, or initiating updates.

```bash
paretosecurity://reset
paretosecurity://showMenu
paretosecurity://update
paretosecurity://welcome
paretosecurity://runChecks
paretosecurity://debug
paretosecurity://logs
```

--------------------------------

### Interactive CLI for Claude Code Configuration

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/README.md

Interactive CLI tool for discovering and managing Claude Code configuration files and slash commands with a beautiful terminal UI.

```CLI
ccexp by nyatinte
```

--------------------------------

### README Generation Configuration

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/HOW_IT_WORKS.md

Illustrates the YAML structure for defining categories used in README generation. Each category includes an ID, display name, prefix, icon, and order for templating.

```yaml
# templates/categories.yaml
categories:
  - id: workflows
    name: "Workflows & Knowledge Guides"
    prefix: wf
    icon: "🧠"
    order: 1
    
  - id: statusline
    name: "Statusline"
    prefix: status
    icon: "📊"
    order: 3
```

--------------------------------

### Manual Testing: Skip Issue Creation

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/scripts/BADGE_AUTOMATION_SETUP.md

Executes the script while skipping the actual creation of GitHub issues. This is achieved by setting the CREATE_ISSUES environment variable to false, allowing for testing of the repository processing logic.

```python
export CREATE_ISSUES=false
python scripts/badge_issue_notification.py
```

--------------------------------

### Adding New Categories Configuration

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/scripts/README.md

Instructions for adding new categories by editing the `templates/categories.yaml` file and updating the issue template. This process is followed by running a generation command.

```yaml
categories:
  - id: cmd
    name: Slash Commands
    prefix: cmd
    icon: "/"
    order: 1
    description: Commands that can be invoked with a slash.
    subcategories:
      - id: cmd-general
        name: General Commands
        prefix: cmd-gen
        icon: ":)"
        order: 1
        description: General purpose commands.
```

--------------------------------

### Husky Git Hooks Setup

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/README.md

Commands for setting up and managing Husky Git hooks to enforce code quality and commit standards.

```bash
# Example for /husky command
# npx husky install
# npx husky add .husky/pre-commit "npm test"
```

--------------------------------

### Build Commands for Guitar Project

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/Guitar/CLAUDE.md

Provides essential commands for setting up dependencies, preparing the environment, and building the Guitar project in both release and debug modes.

```bash
# Setup dependencies
sudo apt install build-essential ruby qmake6 libqt6core6 libqt6gui6 libqt6svg6-dev libqt6core5compat6-dev zlib1g-dev libgl1-mesa-dev libssl-dev
```

```ruby
# Prepare environment
ruby prepare.rb
```

```bash
# Build project
mkdir build && cd build && qmake6 ../Guitar.pro && make -j8
```

```bash
# Release build
qmake "CONFIG+=release" ../Guitar.pro && make
```

```bash
# Debug build
qmake "CONFIG+=debug" ../Guitar.pro && make
```

--------------------------------

### SteadyStart Claude Code Sessions

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/README.md

Clear and direct instructives about style, permissions, Claude's "role", communications, and documentation of Claude Code sessions for other team members to stay abreast.

```Markdown
Clear and direct instructives about style, permissions, Claude's "role", communications, and documentation of Claude Code sessions for other team members to stay abreast.
```

--------------------------------

### Bash Script Execution with Uvicorn

Source: https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code/blob/main/resources/claude.md-files/claude-code-mcp-enhanced/CLAUDE.md

Demonstrates how to run a Python script using 'uvicorn'. It also shows how to set environment variables before executing the script.

```bash
uv run script.py
```

```bash
env VAR_NAME="value" uv run command
```
